meta.title: Humanitarian Visa to Europe — Legal Entry for Protection
meta.description: How to apply for a humanitarian visa at a European embassy to enter legally and claim asylum on arrival — without crossing the Mediterranean.

hero.title: EU Humanitarian Visa
hero.sub: Some EU countries issue humanitarian visas at their embassies, allowing you to travel legally to Europe and claim asylum on arrival — completely avoiding dangerous sea or land crossings. This route is underused and potentially life-saving.

alert.text: A humanitarian visa lets you arrive by plane instead of on a boat. You apply at the embassy in your home country or a third country, and if successful, you fly to Europe legally. This is not widely known — but it is a real option that has saved lives.

what: Under Article 25 of the EU Visa Code, EU member states can issue a "Limited Territorial Validity" (LTV) visa on humanitarian grounds — even when the normal Schengen visa requirements are not met. This allows people facing persecution to travel legally to the issuing country and apply for asylum on arrival.

steps.title: Contact the EU embassy in your current country
steps.desc: Go to or contact the embassy of Portugal, Spain, France, or another EU country where you wish to seek protection. Explain your situation and ask about humanitarian visa possibilities.

steps.title: Prepare your evidence
steps.desc: Document your protection needs clearly: evidence of persecution, threats, medical needs, or extreme vulnerability. Police reports, medical records, witness statements, news coverage of your situation.

steps.title: Submit your application
steps.desc: The embassy will assess your application under Article 25 of the EU Visa Code or national humanitarian provisions. There is no fixed timeline — some decisions come in weeks, others months.

steps.title: Travel to Europe legally
steps.desc: If granted a humanitarian visa, you travel by plane to the issuing EU country. On arrival, you claim asylum in person. Your legal entry significantly strengthens your asylum case.

steps.title: Claim asylum on arrival
steps.desc: At the airport or at a registration office, present yourself and claim asylum. Your humanitarian visa entry is documented — this helps your case. You will be registered and provided with support.

countriesTitle: Which EU countries use this route?

countries.name: Portugal 🇵🇹
countries.detail: Has used Article 25 LTV visas and national humanitarian visa provisions. Portugal's constitution explicitly protects the right to asylum. Known for a relatively accessible humanitarian visa process.

countries.name: Spain 🇪🇸
countries.detail: Offers humanitarian visa provisions under Spanish immigration law. Spanish embassies in conflict-affected countries have issued humanitarian visas. CEAR (Spanish Refugee Council) can advise.

countries.name: France 🇫🇷
countries.detail: Has issued humanitarian visas under Article 25 in specific cases, particularly for persecuted minorities and those with documented threats. OFPRA and La Cimade can advise.

warning: Embassies are not obligated to grant humanitarian visas — they are discretionary. This route requires compelling, documented evidence of protection need. Get legal advice before applying. ECRE and national refugee councils can help.
